question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user set consensusStartTime and consensusEndTime to be equal (endTime == startTime + 1 nanosecond), causing the subscription to immediately complete after a DB query but still allocate all reactive chain resources, and by repeating this at high frequency, cause resource exhaustion through rapid allocation/deallocation cycles?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.fromCallable(() -> toFilter(request)) construction to cause repeated synchronous validation work on the gRPC thread pool by sending many concurrent subscription requests, blocking the thread pool and preventing other gRPC calls from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with startTime far in the past and a very large limit (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE), causing the PollingTopicMessageRetriever to execute Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...) retries on DB errors, holding a scheduler thread in retry backoff loops indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the safetyCheck path (missingMessages with current=null) by subscribing to a topic with a startTime just before a large gap in message sequence numbers, causing the gap recovery query to return a huge result set that overwhelms the reactive pipeline and causes memory exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic with chunked messages where initialTransactionId bytes are malformed, causing parseTransactionID() to throw InvalidProtocolBufferException and log an error for every single message, flooding the logging system and causing I/O saturation on the log output stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open many subscriptions with no endTime and no limit to a topic that receives a continuous high volume of messages, causing the toResponse() map function to be called for every message for every subscriber simultaneously, multiplying CPU usage linearly with subscriber count and causing CPU exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the subscribeTopic() endpoint to send subscription requests faster than the server can process them, causing the gRPC server's internal request queue to grow unboundedly and exhaust heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with a topicID containing an invalid shard/realm/num combination that passes EntityId.of() construction but causes a full table scan in the DB when topicExists() queries EntityRepository.findById(), exhausting DB CPU at high request rates?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with startTime=0 and endTime=Long.MAX_VALUE and limit=0, causing the historical retriever to page through the entire topic message table indefinitely, holding a DB cursor open and consuming a DB connection for the entire duration?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscriberCount is incremented in doOnSubscribe but decremented in doFinally, and by rapidly opening and holding subscriptions, drive subscriberCount to a value that triggers monitoring alerts or causes integer overflow in downstream metric reporting systems?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open many subscriptions to a topic with a startTime in the past, causing each subscription to independently execute the historical retrieval path via topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(filter, true), resulting in N concurrent DB queries (one per subscriber) that overwhelm the database connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) construction by opening many subscriptions to a slow topic, causing each subscription to independently schedule a safetyCheck on Schedulers.boundedElastic() after 1 second, exhausting the bounded elastic thread pool with waiting tasks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where messages have very large chunkTotal values, causing ConsensusMessageChunkInfo.Builder to allocate large proto objects for every message in toResponse(), and by subscribing many times, cause heap exhaustion through accumulated proto object allocation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with a very old startTime and a very large limit, causing the PollingTopicMessageRetriever to execute RepeatSpec with numRepeats=Long.MAX_VALUE polling loops, each issuing a DB query, effectively creating an infinite DB polling loop per subscription?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the concatWith(Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live)) pipeline by opening many subscriptions where historical retrieval never completes (due to continuous new messages), causing the live and safetyCheck fluxes to never be subscribed to, yet holding all allocated resources indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with a valid topicID but with consensusStartTime set to a timestamp that causes DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() to return a value that overflows when used in arithmetic within the retriever (e.g., last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1), causing an infinite loop or incorrect query bounds?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open many subscriptions to different valid topic IDs, causing the topicExists() method to issue a separate EntityRepository.findById() DB query per subscription, and since there is no caching of topic existence, exhaust the DB connection pool with repeated identical lookups?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter(t -> last == null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) predicate by subscribing to a topic where sequence numbers are very large, causing the AtomicReference<TopicMessage> last to hold large TopicMessage objects in memory for every active subscription?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with endTime set to just slightly after startTime on a topic with no messages in that range, causing the pastEndTime() RepeatSpec to poll with Long.MAX_VALUE repeats at the configured endTimeInterval, consuming scheduler resources indefinitely until the interval expires?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that each subscription creates a new TopicContext object with its own AtomicLong count, AtomicReference last, and Stopwatch, and by opening millions of subscriptions, exhaust heap memory through accumulated TopicContext object allocation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription to a topic with a very large number of historical messages and no limit, causing the Flux.fromStream() in the retriever to hold a DB ResultSet open for an extended period, consuming a DB connection and preventing connection pool reuse?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any IP-based or identity-based throttling on subscribeTopic() to open connections from many source ports simultaneously, bypassing any single-connection rate limits and achieving a high aggregate subscription count that exhausts server resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where messages have both large message payloads and large runningHash values, causing toResponse() to call ProtoUtil.toByteString() twice per message per subscriber, and with many concurrent subscribers, cause memory pressure from accumulated ByteString allocations?
